calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jul-23-2019 Off Calendar

Joinder Of Defendant. Byron Ted Field, M.D., To Motion To Change Venue Of Defendants, Dignity Health, U.S. Healthworks Medical Group, Sheila Northcutt, Pt And Lorraine Sunday, M.D.
Off Calendar Mar-26-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT BYRON FIELD'S Joinder To Motion To Change Venue Of Defendants, Dignity Health, U.S. Healthworks Medical Group, Sheila Northcutt, PT And Lorraine Sunday, M.D.

Defendant Byron Ted Field, M.D.'s joinder of motion to change venue is granted. The convenience of the witnesses and judicial economy is furthered by having Plaintiff Robert Rosario's claims against all of the Defendants heard in Orange County.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Change Venue Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Of Defendants, Dignity, U.S. Healthworks Medical Group, Sheila Nothcutt, Pt And Lorraine Sunday, M.D.
Off Calendar Mar-26-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS SHEILA NORTHCUTT, LORRAINE SUNDAY, DIGNITY HEALTH, U.S. HEALTHWORKS MEDICAL GROUP'S Motion To Change Venue Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure.

Defendants Dignity Health, U.S. HealthWorks Medical Group, Sheila Northcutt, P.T., and Lorraine Sunday, M.D.'s motion to change venue pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 397 is granted. While this Court's February 20, 2018 Order denied a previous motion to transfer venue under Code of Civil Procedure Section 395 because venue is proper in San Francisco County, that order specifically preserved the right of Defendants to seek a motion to transfer venue for convenience of the witnesses under Code of Civil Procedure section 397(c).

The convenience of witnesses and the interests of justice support transfer of this action to Orange County. It is undisputed that Defendants Northcutt, Sunday, and Byron Ted Field, M.D. all reside in Orange County and work at a U.S. HealthWorks facility in Orange County. The incident in which Plaintiff was injured occurred at Plaintiff Robert Rosario's place of employment in Anaheim, which is in Orange County. The alleged medical negligence occurred at a facility in Orange County. As supported by Plaintiff's responses to Dr. Field's Interrogatory 6.4, all of Plaintiff's medical care in the wake of the accident occurred in Southern California-in Orange County, Los Angeles County, Riverside County, and San Diego County. (Quilligan Decl. Exh. B, p. 11.) Plaintiff further responded to Interrogatory 2.5 that his residence is in nearby Riverside County. (Id. at p. 4.) Plaintiff's opposition to the motion offers no contrary evidence, and verges on the frivolous.

As in Richfield Hotel Management, Inc. v. Superior Court (1994) 22 Cal.App.4th 222, 227, all of the relevant events took place in another county, all of the witnesses live and work in that county, all of the relevant documents and records are located in that county, and Plaintiff has offered no evidence to suggest that any witnesses would be inconvenienced. It would therefore be an abuse of discretion for the Court to deny this motion where the witnesses would clearly be burdened by the need to travel to San Francisco. (Id.)

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/EPS)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-20-2018 Set For Trial On Aug-05-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Change Venue Of Defendants, Dignity, U.S. Healthworks Medical Group, Sheila Northcutt, Pt And Lorraine Sunday, M.D.
Off Calendar Feb-21-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT SHEILA NORTHCUTT, LORRAINE SUNDAY, DIGNITY HEALTH, U.S. HEALTHWORKS MEDICAL GROUP's Motion To Change Venue Of Defendants, Dignity, U.S. Healthworks Medical Group, Sheila Northcutt, Pt And Lorraine Sunday, M.D.

Defendants' motion for change of venue is denied. Because Dignity Health resides in San Francisco, venue is proper in this county subject to transfer for convenience of the witnesses.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. (D302/HK)
